data.body:
### [2/10] MEASURED FACTS (repo size breakdown; do not re-derive)

- Repo ~443 MB / ~178k objects for ~15k tickets. PACKED SIZE: blobs 424 MB (95.7%), trees 10.7 MB (2.4%), commits 8.2 MB (1.8%). => tree churn / directory sharding / active-vs-archive split are the WRONG levers for SIZE (git deltas trees to ~0; sharding was measured to INCREASE object count 2.00->3.00 trees/commit). Do NOT reopen those.
- `.bridge_state/` = ~375 MB (~85%): bindings.json 219 MB / ~987 committed versions; prev_snapshot.json 155 MB / ~4450 versions; bindings-retired.json ~886 B.
- Actual ticket/event blobs ~35 MB, slow growth.
- CURRENT WORKTREE sizes (measured this session): bindings.json 13.9-14.6 MB across 1235 CONFIRMED bindings (only the bound subset of ~15k tickets); prev_snapshot.json 24.1 MB across ~2050 fetched Jira issues; bindings-retired.json 886 B.
- Serialization is ALREADY canonical for both files: json.dump(indent=2, sort_keys=True), dict-keyed (bindings by local_id; prev by jira_key). Reformatting buys NOTHING; the growth is genuine content churn + over-provisioned payloads (see [4],[5]).
- Nothing reads the git HISTORY of any .bridge_state file — every consumer (reconciler, fsck, identity/authorship trust scan) reads only the CURRENT worktree copy. So bridge_state history's ONLY value is human debugging.

CONSTRAINTS: readers use the WORKTREE not history; cross-clone sync = union-merge of the tickets branch (`.bridge_state/* merge=ours`, local-wins, reconciler rebuilds loser); single-writer via refs/reconciler/lock; the reconciler runs on EPHEMERAL GitHub-Actions runners (*/20 cron) so state must be committed to survive; every write auto-commits+auto-pushes to origin/tickets.
